title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
constraints: k is in the range [1, the number of unique elements in the array]
It is guaranteed that the answer is unique
thinking_questions: How would you count element frequencies efficiently?
Once you have frequencies, how do you get top k without fully sorting?
The maximum frequency is n — can you use that as an array index?

hints.L1: Count frequencies first. Then think about getting the top k without sorting everything — there's a structure designed for 'give me the k largest'.
hints.L2: Count with HashMap, then use bucket sort: bucket[freq] holds all elements with that frequency. Scan from highest freq down, collect k elements.

follow_up_questions: Why does bucket sort work here but not for general sorting?
When would you prefer a heap approach over bucket sort?
What if k equals the number of unique elements?
solution_notes: Bucket sort: O(n). Heap: O(n log k). Key: frequency bounded by n enables bucket sort.
